title: Births, by age of mother
subtitle: Estimated number of births each year by the age of the mother.
caption:
Births, by age of mother

Estimated number of births each year by the age of the mother.

This line chart tracks the estimated number of births worldwide from 1950 to 2023, categorized by the age of the mother. The vertical axis measures the number of births in millions, ranging from 0 to 50 million. The dark blue line, representing mothers aged 20–24 years, begins near 25 million in 1950 and rises to become the highest category for most of the timeline, peaking just below 50 million around 1990 before declining to approximately 35 million by 2023. The red line, indicating mothers aged 25–29 years, starts slightly lower than the 20–24 group but follows a similar upward trajectory, reaching a peak of roughly 40 million in the early 1990s and again in the mid-2010s, before dropping to about 37 million at the end of the period. The purple line for the 30–34 age group shows a steady increase from roughly 16 million in 1950 to a peak of nearly 30 million around 2015, finishing near 28 million. In contrast, the light purple line for the 15–19 age group rises from roughly 16 million to a plateau around 22 million in the late 1960s, then gradually declines to approximately 12 million by 2023. The green line for the 35–39 age group remains relatively flat between 10 and 15 million for several decades before rising to roughly 15 million in recent years. The lines for older age groups (40–44, 45–49, 50–54) and the youngest group (10–14) remain clustered at the bottom of the chart, consistently staying below 5 million throughout the observed timeframe.
